title: Gentianella lehmannii
intro: Gentianella lehmannii, also known as Lehmann's gentianella, is a perennial plant species belonging to the gentian family (Gentianaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that may aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Gentianella lehmannii include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Gentianella lehmannii is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Gentianella lehmannii is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is a member of the Gentianella genus, which is a group of plants commonly known as spring gentians.
